_comment: Seed assumptions for the Mars Manifest Tool. All values are notional order-of-magnitude estimates. Every engine formula must read from here, never hard-code. See HANDOFF.md section 5 & 7.

fleet._affinity_comment: Complementary items bundled 1:1 onto the same hull by the balanced packer: a habitat without its ECLSS (or vice versa) does nothing, so splitting the pair doubles EDL exposure. Test-as-you-fly precedent: every closed-loop life-support demo in history ran inside a sealed volume (NASA LMLSTP chamber demos; ISS ECLSS; MELiSSA; Lunar Palace 1), and inflatables need their own long-duration demo (BEAM 2016). Anti-affinity still spreads the PAIRS across different hulls.

isru._comment: Sabatier-chain rate & energy assumptions (see PROVENANCE.md). Stoichiometry is computed in isru.py from molar masses; these are efficiencies, timing, and the mixture ratio. Energies are system-level (include balance of plant). mode: 'sabatier' (full methalox from water+CO2) or 'oxygen_only' (DRA-5.0-style fallback: LOX from atmospheric CO2 via solid-oxide electrolysis, CH4 imported from Earth -- no water dependency).

edl._comment: Per-ship Entry/Descent/Landing success probability, improving as EDL matures. Musk rated the first uncrewed attempt '50/50'; nothing heavier than ~1 t has ever landed on Mars, so window 0 is genuinely uncertain. success_prob = min(max_prob, base + improvement*synod_index). Tier D judgment; carried as a scenario axis, not a claim.

transport_readiness._comment: Transport-readiness flags: outbound (Handmer's 'orbit, reuse, refill, chill') and the return leg (earth_entry_tps, mars_ascent_tei, surface_prop_transfer). All true in the baseline premise; conservative_feasibility flips the undemonstrated-as-of-2026 ones false to make the DLR/Maiwald critique machine-visible via the L1-TRANS / L1-RET requirements. The return flags gate L1-RET-01 (uncrewed return-flight demo); nothing has yet ascended from Mars, transferred ISRU propellant, or flown an interplanetary-return entry at this scale.

lifecycle._comment: Systems-lifecycle analysis config. risk_weights: relative severity of each gate, normalized ~100, derived from the deep-dive's six hard problems (orbital refueling is Earth-side and outside the surface campaign). crew_era_components: hardware whose function is only exercised when crew arrives; flying it early is idle mass UNLESS it is a demonstration article (demo_exempt_units: first N units count as testbeds, e.g. the 1000-day ECLSS proof).

cost.development_cost_note: Starship $15B+ to date; fission/ISRU/habitat R&D many more $B. Optional separate ledger.

overrides._note: High reuse maturity, pilot-scale ISRU, lean spares.
Near-term launch cost, full-scale ISRU load, heavy spares.
Forces solar; dust-storm battery sizing should surface as a hard feasibility flag.

Internal marginal-cost basis, not customer prices. Cargo ships are EXPENDED on Mars (~$27M ship build per Payload Research + booster amortization + ops ~= $30M); tankers are reused (~$12M/flight at ~10 reuses on a ~$50M build, per Payload Research 2024-25 estimates). See PROVENANCE.md section 4.

NASA Aug-2025 FSP directive (Duffy): >=100 kWe closed-Brayton unit sized for a 15 t heavy-class lander, launch by Q1 FY2030. Mass = lander ceiling (conservative); stowed volume notional. Same 0.15 t/kWe slope as the old 40 kWe/6 t spec.

DRA-5.0-style descope: ISRU makes only LOX from atmospheric CO2 (solid-oxide electrolysis, MOXIE-heritage); methane ships from Earth (~304 t/return load = 3-4 extra cargo ships). Buys out the entire water-mining risk. See docs/COMPARATIVES.md.

Handmer comment-thread fallback: import LH2, make CH4 via Sabatier from local CO2, recycle Sabatier product water for H2, source O2 from CO2 -- no water mining. ~76 t LH2 per 1,400 t return load; volume-bound (~2 ships of LH2) not mass-bound. Trades water-mining risk for surface LH2 cryocooling risk. See docs/CONSIDERED.md, docs/COMPARATIVES.md.

Handmer anchor: ~17 GWh per 240 t CH4 (~70 kWh/kg CH4, ~2x our chain math) -- all-in energies including real-world inefficiencies. Spec energy ~15.2 kWh/kg propellant; full-scale power ~1.7 MW. See docs/COMPARATIVES.md.

The peer-reviewed skeptic case (Maiwald/DLR, Sci Reports 2024): ISRU + orbital refill + cryo-chill are low-TRL and would need 10-20 yr more development than SpaceX plans. Combines Handmer's high ISRU energies, low plant availability/commissioning, heavy spares + AIAA-grade 25% contingency, near-term launch cost, and flips the refill/chill transport-readiness gates FALSE so the L1-TRANS requirements go OPEN. Not a prediction -- the honest lower bound. See docs/COMPARATIVES.md, CITY_RAMP_RESEARCH section 6.

AIAA S-120 mass-growth-allowance by subsystem maturity, replacing the flat 10%: new-technology (ISRU, mining, city industry) 25-30%; new development of an existing type (habitat, fission, site prep, agriculture) 25%; moderate-heritage (robotics, storage, thermal) 20%; mostly-known (comms, consumables, power distribution) 15%; off-the-shelf-ish (science instruments, orbital sats) 10%. The flat 10% baseline is low for TRL 3-4 hardware (flagged in PROVENANCE section 3); this scenario is the honest MGA. Kept a scenario pending review before any baseline promotion.

D4 METHOD DEMONSTRATION, NOT A VALIDATED RESULT. Sizes spares from a Poisson demand model (Owens & de Weck / NASA ICES supportability line): failure_rate = expected failures per unit per synod-length resupply interval; carry the smallest spare count meeting spares_target_reliability. The MATH is rigorous; the failure RATES are D-tier -- anchored loosely to ISS ECLSS heritage (life-support high, fission/passives near-zero) but NOT real Mars MTBFs, which do not exist. Replace the rates when real reliability data arrives; until then informed_spares (per-group fractions from the 34-41% ECLSS band) is the defensible baseline. See docs/CONSIDERED.md.

Literature-informed per-group sparing replacing the flat 35%. Anchors: ECLSS spares 34-41% of system mass for a 919-day Mars mission (Owens/de Weck-line ECLSS reliability analyses; ISS ECLSS = 34% of all logistics mass, NTRS 20210000437) -> 0.40 for life support, and 0.40 for ISRU (same continuous-process class, zero flight heritage). Mechanisms in abrasive regolith 0.30-0.35. FSP reactors are specified for 10 years unattended -> 0.15. Passive distribution/structures 0.10. Groups not listed fall back to spares_fraction_of_dry. See PROVENANCE.md section 3.

The pessimistic water resource: only hydrated/garden regolith (~1-2% water) at the site, no minable clean ice. Per-unit extraction collapses to NASA WER-class ~29 kg/day, making water the program-dominating constraint (tens of units per chain) and slipping crew. The downside the flight-1 minable-ice gate exists to rule out. Analogous to conservative_feasibility.

FROZEN reproduction of the original hand-built spreadsheet (HANDOFF.md 5.1/7): spares 0.35-of-dry, contingency 0.10, pre-2026-07-21 ISRU energies. The workbook-port regression fixtures (tests/test_budgets.py) resolve THIS, not baseline, so the live baseline can be re-anchored to verified data without breaking the port check. Do not edit.

windows._comment: Synod index -> calendar label. Windows recur ~every 26 months. Program start shifted to 2031: no 2026 flight (Moon-first pivot, Feb 2026; orbital refueling undemonstrated mid-2026), first robotic window early 2030s per independent consensus. First crew 2037 remains aggressive vs the 2030s-2040s expert range.

schedule.objective: Loss-tolerant precursor: EDL, power, pilot ISRU, ECLSS testbed
Fuel factory: rate-matched ISRU chain + reactors
Second plant + habitat cluster + deep caches
First crew (gated on capabilities + propellant banked)

capability_gates._comment: Flags unlocked when prerequisite components are installed on the surface (see HANDOFF.md section 6).

capability_unlocks._comment: Data-driven unlock rules evaluated by the CampaignPlanner after each window's deliveries. Conditions in a rule are ANDed: all_of/any_of (component ids delivered), min_installed_kwe, min_landings.
